title: Apply for Aid
text: Duke financial aid applicants complete an aid application and receive and review a financial aid offer with admission. Current Duke students renew their aid applications every year with updated income and asset information. Early Decision applicants should complete the CSS Profile and FAFSA by November 3. Regular Decision applicants should complete the FAFSA and CSS Profile by February 1. If an aid application is not complete by the deadline, total eligibility for aid will not change, but Duke will keep processing applications as they come in, so students should submit as soon as possible to receive an aid offer with the offer of admission.

title: Renew Your Aid
text: Returning students in the 2026-27 school year should submit the renewal aid application before February 1, 2026. For the 2026-27 school year, students share information on the FAFSA and CSS Profile about family income from the 2024 tax year. US citizens and permanent residents should complete the FAFSA, complete the CSS Profile, and monitor the DukeHub To-Do list for additional requested documents. Proxy users cannot upload documents through DukeHub. International students currently receiving financial aid do not need to reapply because aid is renewed automatically each year. International applicants must have checked the financial aid interest box on the original Duke admissions application to receive need-based aid at any point while at Duke.

title: Summer Study Financial Aid
text: Students who receive substantial need-based aid from Duke during the academic year may qualify for assistance during the summer for up to two six-week sessions during their Duke career. Duke calculates summer aid using Cost of Attendance minus Expected Family Contribution equals Need. Summer aid includes a 500 dollar loan and 450 dollars of work study. Students find the summer family contribution by dividing the academic year parent contribution by 4, because the summer session contribution is 25 percent of the academic year parent contribution. For Summer 1, use the previous year's academic year parent contribution. For Summer 2, use the upcoming academic year's contribution, and the financial aid application must be complete to receive Summer 2 aid.

title: Study Away Financial Aid
text: Duke states that students can take financial aid with them for study-away experiences. There are two kinds of academic-year study away programs: Duke-In programs, administered directly by Duke, and Duke-Approved programs, administered by another university but approved for Duke credit. Financial aid works differently between Duke-In and Duke-Approved programs. The page also separates summer Duke-In study away from academic-year study away.

title: Duke Community Standard
text: The Office of Student Conduct and Community Standards strengthens personal responsibility and accountability through investigation and resolution of alleged violations of university policies. Duke University is dedicated to scholarship, leadership, service, honesty, fairness, respect, and accountability. To uphold the Duke Community Standard, students commit that they will not lie, cheat, or steal in academic endeavors; will conduct themselves honorably in all endeavors; and will act if the Standard is compromised. Students and organizations may be held accountable for violations of university policy on or off campus. Undergraduates acknowledge that the university may take disciplinary action, including suspension or expulsion, for failure to abide by regulations or for conduct judged unsatisfactory or detrimental to the university community.

title: Student Responsibility to Duke Policies
text: Duke Community Standard policies emphasize student responsibility to university policies. Duke has high expectations for scholarship and conduct. In accepting admission, students indicate willingness to subscribe to and be governed by university rules and regulations. These policies reflect the Duke Community Standard values of honesty, fairness, respect, and accountability. Students may be accountable for policies included in university materials as well as other conduct judged unsatisfactory or detrimental to the university community.
